tipRules.tip: KVD: 'Fall — burn a spinnerbait just beneath the surface in clear water. Use compact baits with smaller blades (3/16oz, small Colorado/willow or single Colorado) to match the smaller young-of-year baitfish. Keep it in the upper water column.'
KVD: 'In stained fall water, go bigger with Colorado blades for more vibration. For grass like hydrilla/milfoil, use larger baits with willowleaf blades.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Fall spinnerbaits: downsize to 3/16oz in clear water with small blades to match young-of-year baitfish. For heavy grass, go bigger with willowleaf blades. To fish fast and deep, put smaller blades on a 3/4oz head — the Scorcher design lets you burn it and keep it down.'

KVD: 'During fall, lakes become lower and clearer. Baitfish move shallow — great combination for spinnerbaits. The look of spinning blades and vibration really mimics all types of baitfish. Match weight to blades for the right retrieve speed and depth.'
